settings.heading: WATCH OUR STORY
settings.subheading: Hear why I started the company in 60 seconds

settings.text: We engineer athletic greatness through innovation, protection, & performance.

Born out of frustration with outdated, underperforming hockey equipment, Titan BattleGear was created to challenge the status quo.

Mass-market brands treat neck protection as an afterthought. The gear we were forced to wear didn’t reflect the intensity we brought to the game. With decades of expertise in performance apparel and product design, our team set out to create gear that merges cutting-edge technology with sleek, player-focused design.

Built for battle, our cut-resistant products are feature TITANOTEX™, a revolutionary cut-resistant fabric that provides unparalleled safety without compromising mobility. Our patented Stretch ProCurve™ collar deletes velcro entirely from the equation, while our patented Arc~Guard™ design redefines comfort, thanks to its contoured design. Titan BattleGear is the most advanced cut-resistant baselayer, delivering protection with a velcro-free design that adapts to the athlete.

Titan BattleGear brings boldness back to the rink. While the competition continues to churn out uninspired, black baselayer shirts, Titan celebrates the personalities of those that play the game. Our BattleGear stands out with vibrant colors, innovative designs, and a deep respect for the identity of every player and team we serve.

Built for the modern athlete who refuses to settle, we created a baselayer that makes a statement on and off the ice. We’re not here to blend in. We’re here to lead. Titan BattleGear is for the relentless, the fearless, the game-changers.

Together We Conquer...

As a hockey parent, the tragic incidents in the sport of hockey compelled me to seek out effective neck laceration protection for my own child, only to discover a glaring gap in the market.

This realization sparked my mission to create Titan BattleGear, to innovate protective gear, and set an entirely new standard for safety, performance, and style for players at every level.

We engineer champions through innovation, protection, and performance. Our mission is simple: "Engineer Athletic Greatness." We see challenges as opportunities and craft gear that inspires fearless performance.

Our mantra, "Together We Conquer," reflects our belief in unity and shared success. Titan BattleGear empowers athletes to push limits, redefine greatness, and pursue excellence with unwavering confidence.

settings.heading: Andries de Villiers, Founder & CEO
settings.text:
Andries is an entrepreneur, investor, visionary, with nearly two decades of leadership experience. He excels at building and scaling startups to deliver multibillion-dollar outcomes.

As a hockey parent, he saw a huge gap in the market in terms of safety, performance, and style. The alarming need to do better was evident after taking a dull knife to existing neck guards, and realizing the superficial level of safety provided by the mass-market brands was not keeping his kid safe on the ice.

Prior to launching Titan, he served as Chief Growth Officer at Majestic Steel, and various other C-level roles at multi-billion dollar companies to early stage startups.

Outside the office, Andries enjoys playing hockey, traveling to tournaments with his son, cycling, golfing, and spending time with family and friends.

settings.heading: Adam Clement, Co-Founder & Chief Product & Design Officer
settings.text:
Adam Clement is a creative leader with over 25 years of experience in design and branding. A former Vice President of Design at Under Armour, Adam spent nearly 17 years at the company, where he oversaw multiple categories including team sports, men’s training, and boy’s training. While at UA, he worked with major universities such as Notre Dame, UCLA, and Wisconsin, as well as professional teams including Tottenham Hotspur and the Welsh Rugby Union. His designs have been featured on ESPN, NBC, and other major media outlets, reaching audiences nationwide.

Prior to Under Armour, Adam spent six years at Widmeyer Communications, specializing in logo design and corporate branding. He later served as the Executive Creative Director at BlueChip Holdings, and as VP of Design and Creative at Origin USA, where he directed all design efforts.

Outside of work, Adam (a goalie) is a passionate hockey player, regularly competing against top players in Maryland. As dedicated head coach, he leads his son’s 12U travel hockey team and serves on the board of the Baltimore Youth Hockey Club.

settings.heading: Chris Wideman, Strategic Advisor
settings.text:
Chris Wideman is an American former professional ice hockey defenseman. He was selected in the fourth round, 100th overall, by the Ottawa Senators in the 2009 NHL entry draft. Wideman played for the Senators, Edmonton Oilers, Florida Panthers, and Montreal Canadiens in the National Hockey League (NHL).

As a former NHL defenseman turned entrepreneur and investor, and known for his leadership on and off the ice, Chris now applies that same focus and drive to building businesses. His portfolio spans franchises, real estate, hockey equipment, and energy investments. As a father of two and a passionate operator, Chris is dedicated to scaling ventures that combine smart strategy, operational excellence, and long-term value creation.

At Titan, Chris primarily oversees player and league relationships, as well as playing a critical role in product development and on-ice testing.

settings.heading: Dion Phaneuf, Strategic Advisor
settings.text:
Dion Phaneuf is a former NHL defenseman, three-time NHL All-Star, and one of the most recognized leaders of his era. Over a 14-season NHL career, Phaneuf played more than 1,000 games for the Calgary Flames, Toronto Maple Leafs, Ottawa Senators, and Los Angeles Kings, earning a reputation for his physical style of play, leadership, and relentless competitiveness.

Drafted 9th overall in the 2003 NHL Draft by the Calgary Flames, Phaneuf made an immediate impact in the league, becoming a Calder Trophy finalist in his rookie season and later a Norris Trophy finalist as one of the NHL’s top defensemen. He served as captain of the Toronto Maple Leafs and represented Team Canada on the international stage, winning gold medals at the IIHF World Junior Championship and IIHF World Championship.

Known throughout his career for his toughness, intensity, and commitment to the game, Phaneuf brings deep experience across elite performance, leadership, team culture, and player development. As a member of Titan BattleGear’s Strategic Advisory Board, he supports the company’s mission to push hockey protection forward through innovation, performance, and authenticity to the next generation of players.

settings.heading: William Sirignano, Board Member
settings.text:
William is senior investment professional, manager and dealmaker with over 18 years of Wall Street, hedge fund and private credit professional experience. His areas of expertise include capital markets and risk underwriting as well as sourcing, negotiating and structuring complex deals.

As an Operating Partner of Mission 3A, William leads Mission 3A's investment in Titan Battlegear.

William spent 11 years at Blackstone where he was a Managing Director, during which time he sourced >$5 billion worth of private corporate credit deals and co-managed a $3.5 billion opportunistic corporate credit portfolio.

William is a passionate and serial venture and seed investor who prefers to take a proactive role as an advisor and partner to the companies in which he invests.

He has served on the Board of Directors for several companies, totaling >$2 billion in sales. William received a Bachelor of Arts degree Cum Laude in Economics from Williams College also studying corporate finance and mathematics.

settings.heading: LEADERSHIP
settings.description: Behind every great innovation is a team of passionate individuals. Get to know the faces driving our mission to redefine performance, protection, and style in hockey.

settings.heading: Andries de Villiers, Founder & CEO
settings.text:
Andries is an entrepreneur, investor, visionary, and C-level executive with nearly two decades of leadership experience. He excels at building and scaling startups to deliver multibillion-dollar outcomes.

As a hockey parent, he saw a huge gap in the market in terms of safety, performance, and style. The alarming need to do better was evident after taking a dull knife to existing neck guards, and realizing the superficial level of safety provided by the mass-market brands was not keeping his kid safe on the ice.

Prior to launching Titan, he served as Chief Growth Officer at Majestic Steel, driving digital transformation and strategic initiatives.

Outside the office, Andries enjoys playing hockey, traveling to tournaments with his son, cycling, golfing, and spending time with family and friends.

settings.heading: Adam Clement, Chief Product Officer
settings.text:
Adam Clement is a creative leader with over 25 years of experience in design and branding. A former Vice President of Design at Under Armour, Adam spent nearly 17 years at the company, where he oversaw multiple categories including team sports, men’s training, and boy’s training. While at UA, he worked with major universities such as Notre Dame, UCLA, and Wisconsin, as well as professional teams including Tottenham Hotspur and the Welsh Rugby Union. His designs have been featured on ESPN, NBC, and other major media outlets, reaching audiences nationwide.

Prior to Under Armour, Adam spent six years at Widmeyer Communications, specializing in logo design and corporate branding. He later served as the Executive Creative Director at BlueChip Holdings, and as VP of Design and Creative at Origin USA, where he directed all design efforts.

Outside of work, Adam (a goalie) is a passionate hockey player, regularly competing against top players in Maryland. As dedicated head coach, he leads his son’s 12U travel hockey team and serves on the board of the Baltimore Youth Hockey Club.

settings.heading: Douglas Lein, Chief Business Officer
settings.text:
Doug has a passion for breaking the trend through innovative business investments. Having served in many different capacities from the ground floor to executive level positions, he has fostered an eclectic business acumen. With over 35 years of hockey experience, from playing, coaching and team ownership, his vast knowledge and connections throughout the sports world brings a unique perspective to Titan.

He is the owner of a Canadian Junior A Hockey Club as well as serving on the league Executive Committee as The Chairman of the Board for the last 6 years.

Doug has spent time serving at the national level with the Canadian Junior Hockey League as well other executive sub-groups.

Doug founded and manages an equity group with holdings in the sports world as well as a wide range from start-ups, to long established family-owned businesses. Outside of hockey and his work life, Doug enjoys spending his time with family, his wife Sophia, their daughter Winifred, their dog Oakley, along with a close group of friends. His free-time activities include traveling, boating, waterskiing, golf, hunting, fishing, and snowmobiling.

settings.heading: Nicolas Krauss, Chief Growth Officer
settings.text:
Nicolas Krauss is an entrepreneur with a strong track record in building businesses and driving growth. Before joining Titan Battlegear, Nicolas was the Founder & CEO of dasFlow Custom Sublimation Apparel, a leading manufacturer of high-performance, full dye-sublimation apparel. At dasFlow, he combined cutting-edge fabric technology with innovative design, delivering exceptional comfort, quality, and durability to customers.

Nicolas’s connection to Titan Battlegear began as a client and vendor, where he recognized the company's transformative potential in redefining performance safety gear and apparel. This partnership inspired him to dedicate his expertise to advancing the company's success.

Earlier in his career, Nicolas gained valuable experience as a business consultant for a global real estate franchising network and operated a small real estate investment firm. His diverse background reflects his entrepreneurial spirit and dedication to excellence. In addition to his professional achievements, Nicolas is multilingual, speaking English, German, Spanish, and Portuguese fluently.

Outside of work, Nicolas is passionate about staying active and enjoying life outdoors. He is an avid soccer player, triathlete, and fitness enthusiast who also enjoys gardening and spending sunny days with family and friends.
